briefdescription.para: Coefficient table for 4-tap vertical chroma resampling conversions.

detaileddescription.para: This 3D array contains the filter coefficients used for vertical chroma resampling between different chroma subsampling formats (e.g., 4:2:2 to 4:2:0 and vice versa).

Dimensions:
[XV_VCRSMPLR_NUM_CONVERSIONS]: Number of conversion types (e.g., 422->420, 420->422).
[XV_VCRSMPLR_MAX_PHASES]: Number of filter phases per conversion.
[XV_VCRSMPLR_TAPS_4]: Number of taps (coefficients) per phase (4-tap filter).
Each entry contains the coefficients for a specific conversion and phase.

briefdescription.para: Coefficient table for vertical chroma resampler with 6 taps.

detaileddescription.para:
This 3D array contains precomputed filter coefficients used by the vertical chroma resampler for different conversion modes and phases. The coefficients are organized as follows:
First dimension: Conversion type (e.g., 422->420, 420->422)
Second dimension: Phase of the resampling process
Third dimension: Tap coefficients for the filter (6 taps per phase)
The coefficients are used to perform chroma resampling between different chroma subsampling formats.

briefdescription.para: Coefficient table for vertical chroma resampling with 8 taps.

detaileddescription.para: This 3D array contains precomputed filter coefficients used for vertical chroma resampling between different chroma subsampling formats (e.g., 4:2:2 to 4:2:0 and vice versa).

Dimensions:
[XV_VCRSMPLR_NUM_CONVERSIONS]: Number of conversion types (e.g., 422->420, 420->422).
[XV_VCRSMPLR_MAX_PHASES]: Number of filter phases.
[XV_VCRSMPLR_TAPS_8]: Number of taps per filter (8 taps).
Each entry contains the coefficients for a specific conversion and phase.

Example conversions:
422->420: Downsampling from 4:2:2 to 4:2:0 chroma format.
420->422: Upsampling from 4:2:0 to 4:2:2 chroma format.

briefdescription.para: Coefficient table for vertical chroma resampler with 10 taps.

detaileddescription.para:
This 3D array contains precomputed filter coefficients for chroma resampling conversions between 4:2:2 and 4:2:0 formats. The coefficients are organized as:
First dimension: Conversion type (e.g., 422->420, 420->422)
Second dimension: Filter phase
Third dimension: Tap index (10 taps per filter)
The coefficients are used by the vertical chroma resampler hardware or software to perform high-quality chroma sub-sampling or up-sampling.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the core registers with computed coefficients for required conversion.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is instance id of the core

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if device is found and initialized XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if device is not found

briefdescription.para: This function starts the Chroma resampler core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the Chroma resampler core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function loads default filter coefficients in the chroma resampler coefficient storage based on the selected TAP configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function loads user defined filter coefficients in the horiz.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to user defined filter coefficients table

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Chroma resampler active resolution.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Chroma resampler for the required format conversion.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function prints Chroma Resampler status on the console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
